item.title: auto --limit N flag to cap items processed per session
item.intent: Users must be able to cap an auto session at N items — auto done --limit 3 processes exactly 3 items then stops cleanly, regardless of how many ready items remain. Clean exit, not a circuit-breaker failure.
item.description: Users running auto done on a large backlog sometimes want to run a bounded session: test a new config on 2 items, manage API budget, or batch work into controlled chunks. The current auto command runs until the backlog drains or circuit-breaker fires. A --limit N flag adds an explicit session cap that exits cleanly (not as a failure) after N items complete. Distinct from --max-failures (circuit breaker) and priority scope (p0/p1/p2) — this is a pure count cap.
steps: Add --limit <N> flag to cmd_auto option parsing with validate_arg for non-empty positive integer
Pass limit through to run_sprint_loop; track items_completed counter incremented on each sprint success
When items_completed reaches limit, exit sprint loop cleanly with log_success 'Session limit reached: N items completed'
Limit exit is not counted as a failure — circuit breaker consecutive_failures counter is not incremented
Update help text for auto command to document --limit flag
acceptanceCriteria: auto done --limit 2 processes exactly 2 items then exits with a clean success message
The remaining backlog items are untouched after a --limit exit
auto done --limit 0 exits immediately with a clear 'limit must be a positive integer' error
Circuit-breaker consecutive_failures counter is not affected by a limit exit
help text shows --limit flag under auto command
